items.title: Today the Summer Sixers face the Pacers, and former two-way player Alex Reese.
items.author: Gina Mizell
items.body_excerpt:
Today the Summer Sixers face the Pacers, and former two-way player Alex Reese. 

Do you remember him and his unconventional jersey number? Last season, @adamaaronson.bsky.social and I happened to ask about it, and he told us both about his mom. 

www.inquirer.com/sixers/alex-...

media.title: Alex Reese wears No. 65 to honor his late mother, including in the Pacers’ Summer League game vs. his old team
media.description: Reese, who played 14 games with the Sixers two seasons ago, chose the number because it aligns with the birth year of his mother, Rebakah, who died in 2024 from early-onset Alzheimer's disease.

items.author: Adam Aaronson
items.body_excerpt:
New: Today, the Sixers face Alex Reese. You may remember his odd jersey number.

Glad to finally be able to share this: days before being waived last July, Reese told @ginamizell.bsky.social and me the story of love and grief behind the No. 65 still on his back:
www.phillyvoice.com/sixers-news-...

media.title: Former Sixers forward Alex Reese honors late mother with unique jersey number: 'It just helps me think of her'
media.description: During his time with the Sixers, Alex Reese became the second player in NBA history – and the first since 1950 – to wear 65 on his jersey. It is much more than an oddity.

items.title: Hollander POTD Rod Strickland. NYC PG played at DePaul, drafted 19th by NY in 88. Logjam w/MJax got Rod traded to SA. Had issues in SA & let go to POR in 92, where he was great but clashed w/PJ. Played w/WAS & others thru 05. Led NBA in APG in 97-98 was somehow never an All-Star.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst
items.body_excerpt: Hollander POTD Rod Strickland. NYC PG played at DePaul, drafted 19th by NY in 88. Logjam w/MJax got Rod traded to SA. Had issues in SA & let go to POR in 92, where he was great but clashed w/PJ. Played w/WAS & others thru 05. Led NBA in APG in 97-98 was somehow never an All-Star.
